1: A revelation of God: "Make a joyful noise to the LORD, all the earth!" The universal scope of worship — all the earth — is the scope of the gospel. Revelation 7:9-10 — "a great multitude that no one could number, from every nation, from all tribes and peoples and languages, standing before the throne and before the Lamb, crying out with a loud voice." The joyful noise of all the earth is the worship before Christ's throne.
2: A revelation of God: "Serve the LORD with gladness! Come into his presence with singing!" Ephesians 2:18 — "through him we both have access in one Spirit to the Father." Hebrews 4:16 — "let us then with confidence draw near to the throne of grace." The access to God's presence that Ps 100 calls for is the access Christ opens — through him, with gladness, we come into the Father's presence.
3: A direct: "Know that the LORD, he is God! It is he who made us, and we are his; we are his people, and the sheep of his pasture." John 10:14,27 — "I am the good shepherd. I know my own and my own know me... My sheep hear my voice, and I know them, and they follow me." The knowledge of the Lord and the identity as his sheep are fulfilled in the relationship of Christ and his flock. To know that the LORD is God is to know that Jesus is Lord (John 20:28).
4: A type: "Enter his gates with thanksgiving, and his courts with praise!" John 10:9 — "I am the gate; whoever enters through me will be saved." Hebrews 10:19-22 — "we have confidence to enter the holy places by the blood of Jesus, by the new and living way that he opened for us through the curtain, that is, through his flesh." The gates we enter are entered through Christ; the thanksgiving we bring is for his sacrifice.
5: A revelation of God: "For the LORD is good; his steadfast love endures forever, and his faithfulness to all generations." John 1:17 — "grace and truth came through Jesus Christ." Ephesians 2:7 — "the immeasurable riches of his grace in kindness toward us in Christ Jesus." The goodness, steadfast love, and faithfulness that are "forever" and "to all generations" are the attributes of God most fully displayed in Christ — in the cross (Romans 5:8) and in the promise of his return.
